Center: Russian Nuclear Structure and Reaction Data Centre, Kurchatov Institute, Moscow, Russia
ref: Jour: Bull.Russian Academy of Sciences - Physics, Vol.42, Issue.11, p.132 (1978)
title: Cross section of the reaction (alpha,xn) on the nuclei 154Gd and 160Gd in the energy interval E(alpha)=47-130 MeV

x4freetext: Cross section of the reaction (alpha,xn) on the
nuclei 154Gd and 160Gd in the energy interval

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Bull.Russian Academy of Sciences - Physics, Vol.42, Issue.11, p.132 (1978)
Jour: Izv. Rossiiskoi Akademii Nauk, Ser.Fiz., Vol.42, p.2369 (1978)

x4codes.hlp: Instytut Badan Jadrowych, Swierk and Warszawa, Poland

x4freetext: Corrections on summing-up, electronic dead time,
angular distribution of gamma-rays, self absorption in
KX-rays produced in the Gd-targets were used to
normalize the yields to the beam flux.
Several Ge(Li) detectors with active volume of
Monitor X-ray spectra were measured with an
intrinsic germanium detector of 10 mm diameter and 5 mm
depth and with a `FWHM` of about 500 eV at 122 keV
The dominant contribution to the total error
of the cross section is made by the calibration of
The error of the normalization parameter

x4code.shortRef: Jour: Bull.Russian Academy of Sciences - Physics, Vol.42, Issue.11, p.132 (1978)

x4freetext: Tbls 1, 2. Several values given in the publication
were superseded or corrected by D.Chmielewska.

x4code.actHelp: Data received at the data center

x4freetext: Corrections were made according to new EXFOR rules.

2nd partial uncertainty, defined under ERR-ANALYS

reacs.QuantHlp: Cross section

x4freetext: Derived from gamma production cross sections
considering the side feeding deduced from the level
The side-feed radiation correction varies from 20%
near the reaction threshold to a few percent at high
excitation energies. Detailed information on the
extrapolation procedure that leads to the
determination of this correction and its errors is
given by Chmielewska, etc. (see Nukleonika, v.23, p.333
